paper_title_working: Effects of DOI on cortical network activity measured by multi-electrode array

affiliations.institute: Center for Sleep and Consciousness / Wisconsin Institute for Sleep and Consciousness

authors.role: Lead author; designed and ran the MEA experiments, performed analysis, and drafted the manuscript
authors.notes: PhD candidate in BME, co-advised by Aviad Hai and Giulio Tononi. Research focus on neural correlates of consciousness, electrophysiology, MEA cultures, and altered states. Listed on both Hai Lab and Center for Sleep and Consciousness people pages.

affiliations.department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering
affiliations.institute: Wisconsin Institute for Translational Neuroengineering (WITNe) / Wisconsin Institutes for Discovery

authors.role: Developed the MEA platform and primary cortical neuron plating protocol used in this study
authors.notes: ECE PhD '24 alumnus of the Hai Lab. Authored the in-house MEA plating protocol document used by the lab. Now postdoctoral researcher (Belton Lab). ORCID confirmed via OpenAlex profile.

authors.role: Built the original code infrastructure for processing and analyzing MEA recordings from the psychoplastogen project
authors.notes: BME PhD at UW–Madison; worked in the Hai Lab on analysis pipelines for the psychoplastogen MEA dataset. Also did prior work in the Saha Lab on CAR T cells. The legacy codebase under /Users/bkutler4/... is from him. OpenAlex shows a single CAR-T paper under his name and confirms UW–Madison affiliation.

authors.role: Undergraduate researcher; assisted with MEA experiments and characterization of psychedelic effects on neuronal cultures
authors.notes: Undergraduate researcher (BME '26) in the Hai Lab, focused on studying psychedelic effects on neuronal plasticity using MEAs and large-scale calcium imaging. Spelling corrected to 'Nornberg' per v1.7 docx. No public ORCID or email found.

affiliations.institute: School of Medicine and Public Health
School of Medicine and Public Health

authors.role: Collaborator providing expertise on cortical network electrophysiology, consciousness, and psychedelic mechanisms
authors.notes: Professor of Anesthesiology and Affiliate Professor of Neuroscience, UW–Madison. PhD in Neuroscience (UW). Lab investigates anesthesia, sleep, consciousness, and psychedelic mechanisms — directly relevant to a DOI/ketanserin MEA study. ORCID found via Google search; not directly verified on his department biosketch page.

affiliations.institute: Wisconsin Institute for Sleep and Consciousness / Center for Sleep and Consciousness

authors.role: Co-advisor of Ido Haber; collaborator providing conceptual framing on consciousness and integrated information theory
authors.notes: Distinguished Professor in Consciousness Science, David P. White Chair in Sleep Medicine; Director, Wisconsin Institute for Sleep and Consciousness; UW Dept. of Psychiatry. Address: 6001 Research Park Blvd, Madison, WI 53719. ORCID from OpenAlex.

affiliations.institute: Wisconsin Institute for Translational Neuroengineering (WITNe) / Wisconsin Institutes for Discovery / Grainger Institute for Engineering

affiliations.department: Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering (affiliated)

authors.role: Senior author and PI; supervised the project, provided lab resources, and led manuscript preparation
authors.notes: Vilas Early Career Associate Professor, Department of Biomedical Engineering, UW–Madison. PI of Hai Lab. Office: 2112 Engineering Centers Building, 1550 Engineering Dr, Madison, WI 53706. Phone: (608) 890-3411. ORCID confirmed from his ORCID profile and OpenAlex.
